task.summary: - Restructured graphing views to reuse the same component - Made the graphing views draggable and zoomable via mouse wheel
- Cleanup and a bit of restructuring - Optimizations to how things load - Rework of some ui
- Stats view - Attribute view - updated README.md
- Updated README.md - Updated shortcuts dialogue - cleanup
- ui cleanup and improvements - xref fix for overloads - xref fix for bootstrap handles and nested bootstraps - other various bug fixes
- class creation - resource file importing/creation - class/resource deletion - fixed a caching bug in decompilation
- Added refresh button to the bytecode debugger

Fix recompiled changes not persisting to bytecode/export and surface results in the editor: - Per-method resilience: a method that can't lower keeps its original bytecode and reports a warning instead of aborting the whole class - Refresh all open views (bytecode, const-pool, hex, IR, AST, ...) after a successful recompile - Add new methods/fields and regenerate <clinit> from static initializers - Remove methods dropped from source (constructors/synthetics guarded) - Materialize synthetic lambdas (rolls back on failure)
LLVM Support: - View support for LLVM IR as a View
- support for changing class level of created classes
- Can create new classes from blank project now
- Improved bytecode view - fixed highlight bug in bytecode view - Auto update checking now

Rewrite Query Explorer DSL as a composable matcher algebra - Replace the per-predicate query AST/visitors with a composable, registry-driven Condition/Accessor/Operator algebra - Add static, SSA/CFG (recursive, loops, inLoop, loopDepth), and data-flow (flowsTo/flowsFrom over param/return/arg/insn) tiers - Add SEQUENCE [...] instruction-pattern matching (opcode/_/.. steps, ( ) predicates, * + {n,m}) plus an opcodes regex shorthand - Move the Query Explorer from a popup into a right-edge tool-window tab (new ToolWindowPane), keeping the inspector as the default tab - Add DSL syntax highlighting (QueryTokenMaker: keywords, operators, literals, opcode mnemonics) and a themed HTML help popup with highlighted examples - Remove the visual query builder and the Query Explorer menu entry - Add WrapLayout so the query config controls stay visible in narrow widths - Remove the standalone Dependencies and Data Flow analysis panels; restructure the Analysis menu (Simulation -> Code Analysis; add Similarity/Search/Strings) - Add query test coverage (pipeline, SSA, data-flow, sequence, tokenizer, help examples); - Cleaned up a LOT of dead/old code - bumped version to 13
- Fixed a bug with query DSLs `IN` sets - Updated some stale data in README
- Migrated Query Engine over to YABR

- Extract non-UI logic out of com.tonic.ui into sibling packages (util/model/event/service/deobfuscation/script/simulation); cli/plugin now depend on those, not ui - Decouple entry-model icons to string keys resolved UI-side - Migrate query UI to YABR's com.tonic.analysis.query engine (QueryMatch/QueryTarget); build result labels UI-side - Clear query results when a new project loads - Default the bytecode debugger's "Recursive Calls" checkbox to on - Migrated Query Engine over to YABR

- Find Usages now jumps to the exact call site in source and selects the referenced name - Bytecode debugger: tabbed Bytecode/Source view; source tab tracks the executing line and supports breakpoints (gutter click or context menu) - Debugger source tab shows only the current method (exact decompiler spans, editor-matching line numbers) - Debugger opens in a frame with minimize/maximize - Fix breakpoints binding to the wrong method when viewing callees in recursive mode
- Version bump 16.0 - Fix NPE scrolling the debugger source tab before the window is laid out
- Version bump 17.0 - Add Dual view: linked bytecode/source panes

Add usage-count lenses (class/method/field) + span-based navigation - Clickable "N usages" lenses on class, method, and field declarations; open Find Usages (View ▸ Usage Counts toggle) - Shared XrefQueryService (one xref build + filter for lenses and Find Usages); class usages scoped to type references, not member accesses - Navigate/locate declarations via decompiler spans; regex kept only as Hide-Annotations fallback - Find Usages selection skips string/char literals and prefers the real member-access/call token - Recompiling a class invalidates the xref database

Add JStudio Live: attach to a running JVM New :live-agent and :live-client subprojects - Attach to a running JVM and browse its loaded classes - Patch & Continue: recompile the current class and live-redefine it, grafting only the edited method bodies so the member set is preserved - Capture runtime-defined classes into the project - Heap instances, static fields, and thread/deadlock views
Also: live class enumeration/capture in ProjectService (filtering JVM-internal classes), a scripting bridge, and Live menu/toolbar wiring.

Add JStudio Live: attach to a running JVM New :live-agent and :live-client subprojects - Attach to a running JVM and browse its loaded classes - Patch & Continue: recompile the current class and live-redefine it, grafting only the edited method bodies so the member set is preserved - Capture runtime-defined classes into the project - Heap instances, static fields, and thread/deadlock views
Also: live class enumeration/capture in ProjectService (filtering JVM-internal classes), a scripting bridge, and Live menu/toolbar wiring.

MESSAGE.value:
Fix recompiled changes not persisting to bytecode/export and surface results in the editor: - Per-method resilience: a method that can't lower keeps its original bytecode and reports a warning instead of aborting the whole class - Refresh all open views (bytecode, const-pool, hex, IR, AST, ...) after a successful recompile - Add new methods/fields and regenerate <clinit> from static initializers - Remove methods dropped from source (constructors/synthetics guarded) - Materialize synthetic lambdas (rolls back on failure)
LLVM Support: - View support for LLVM IR as a View
- support for changing class level of created classes
- Can create new classes from blank project now
- Improved bytecode view - fixed highlight bug in bytecode view
- Improved bytecode view - fixed highlight bug in bytecode view - Auto update checking now
- Small tweak to self-update/update check
- Undid update check throttling
- Bump version to 11

Rewrite Query Explorer DSL as a composable matcher algebra - Replace the per-predicate query AST and planner visitors with a composable, registry-driven Condition/Accessor/Operator algebra - Add static, SSA/CFG (recursive, loops, inLoop, loopDepth), and data-flow (flowsTo/flowsFrom over param/return/arg/insn) query tiers - Add syntax highlighting to the DSL editor via a dedicated QueryTokenMaker - Rebuild the query help popup as themed HTML with per-example syntax-highlighted boxes - Rewrite the visual query builder to emit composable DSL instead of the removed predicate syntax - Add query tests covering the parse→plan→run pipeline, SSA/data-flow, and the builder/help syntax contracts - Remove the standalone Dependencies and Data Flow analysis panels - Restructure the Analysis menu (rename Simulation to Code Analysis; add Similarity, Search, and Strings entries)

Rewrite Query Explorer DSL as a composable matcher algebra - Replace the per-predicate query AST/visitors with a composable, registry-driven Condition/Accessor/Operator algebra - Add static, SSA/CFG (recursive, loops, inLoop, loopDepth), and data-flow (flowsTo/flowsFrom over param/return/arg/insn) tiers - Add SEQUENCE [...] instruction-pattern matching (opcode/_/.. steps, ( ) predicates, * + {n,m}) plus an opcodes regex shorthand - Move the Query Explorer from a popup into a right-edge tool-window tab (new ToolWindowPane), keeping the inspector as the default tab - Add DSL syntax highlighting (QueryTokenMaker: keywords, operators, literals, opcode mnemonics) and a themed HTML help popup with highlighted examples - Remove the visual query builder and the Query Explorer menu entry - Add WrapLayout so the query config controls stay visible in narrow widths - Remove the standalone Dependencies and Data Flow analysis panels; restructure the Analysis menu (Simulation -> Code Analysis; add Similarity/Search/Strings) - Add query test coverage (pipeline, SSA, data-flow, sequence, tokenizer, help examples);

Rewrite Query Explorer DSL as a composable matcher algebra - Replace the per-predicate query AST/visitors with a composable, registry-driven Condition/Accessor/Operator algebra - Add static, SSA/CFG (recursive, loops, inLoop, loopDepth), and data-flow (flowsTo/flowsFrom over param/return/arg/insn) tiers - Add SEQUENCE [...] instruction-pattern matching (opcode/_/.. steps, ( ) predicates, * + {n,m}) plus an opcodes regex shorthand - Move the Query Explorer from a popup into a right-edge tool-window tab (new ToolWindowPane), keeping the inspector as the default tab - Add DSL syntax highlighting (QueryTokenMaker: keywords, operators, literals, opcode mnemonics) and a themed HTML help popup with highlighted examples - Remove the visual query builder and the Query Explorer menu entry - Add WrapLayout so the query config controls stay visible in narrow widths - Remove the standalone Dependencies and Data Flow analysis panels; restructure the Analysis menu (Simulation -> Code Analysis; add Similarity/Search/Strings) - Add query test coverage (pipeline, SSA, data-flow, sequence, tokenizer, help examples); - Cleaned up a LOT of dead/old code

Rewrite Query Explorer DSL as a composable matcher algebra - Replace the per-predicate query AST/visitors with a composable, registry-driven Condition/Accessor/Operator algebra - Add static, SSA/CFG (recursive, loops, inLoop, loopDepth), and data-flow (flowsTo/flowsFrom over param/return/arg/insn) tiers - Add SEQUENCE [...] instruction-pattern matching (opcode/_/.. steps, ( ) predicates, * + {n,m}) plus an opcodes regex shorthand - Move the Query Explorer from a popup into a right-edge tool-window tab (new ToolWindowPane), keeping the inspector as the default tab - Add DSL syntax highlighting (QueryTokenMaker: keywords, operators, literals, opcode mnemonics) and a themed HTML help popup with highlighted examples - Remove the visual query builder and the Query Explorer menu entry - Add WrapLayout so the query config controls stay visible in narrow widths - Remove the standalone Dependencies and Data Flow analysis panels; restructure the Analysis menu (Simulation -> Code Analysis; add Similarity/Search/Strings) - Add query test coverage (pipeline, SSA, data-flow, sequence, tokenizer, help examples); - Cleaned up a LOT of dead/old code - bumped version to 13
- Query DSL md docs
- Fixed a bug with query DSLs `IN` sets - Updated some stale data in README
- Migrated Query Engine over to YABR

- Extract non-UI logic out of com.tonic.ui into sibling packages (util/model/event/service/deobfuscation/script/simulation); cli/plugin now depend on those, not ui - Decouple entry-model icons to string keys resolved UI-side - Migrate query UI to YABR's com.tonic.analysis.query engine (QueryMatch/QueryTarget); build result labels UI-side - Clear query results when a new project loads - Default the bytecode debugger's "Recursive Calls" checkbox to on - Migrated Query Engine over to YABR

- Find Usages now jumps to the exact call site in source and selects the referenced name - Bytecode debugger: tabbed Bytecode/Source view; source tab tracks the executing line and supports breakpoints (gutter click or context menu) - Debugger source tab shows only the current method (exact decompiler spans, editor-matching line numbers) - Debugger opens in a frame with minimize/maximize - Fix breakpoints binding to the wrong method when viewing callees in recursive mode
- Version bump 15.0
- Version bump 16.0 - Fix NPE scrolling the debugger source tab before the window is laid out
- Version bump 17.0 - Add Dual view: linked bytecode/source panes

Add usage-count lenses (class/method/field) + span-based navigation - Clickable "N usages" lenses on class, method, and field declarations; open Find Usages (View ▸ Usage Counts toggle) - Shared XrefQueryService (one xref build + filter for lenses and Find Usages); class usages scoped to type references, not member accesses - Navigate/locate declarations via decompiler spans; regex kept only as Hide-Annotations fallback - Find Usages selection skips string/char literals and prefers the real member-access/call token - Recompiling a class invalidates the xref database
- Cleanup
- Update ReadMe

Add JStudio Live: attach to a running JVM New :live-agent and :live-client subprojects - Attach to a running JVM and browse its loaded classes - Patch & Continue: recompile the current class and live-redefine it, grafting only the edited method bodies so the member set is preserved - Capture runtime-defined classes into the project - Heap instances, static fields, and thread/deadlock views
Also: live class enumeration/capture in ProjectService (filtering JVM-internal classes), a scripting bridge, and Live menu/toolbar wiring.
- Version bump
